title: arXiv external grounding sweep for worst-grounded PHIL claims
motivation: Grounding audit shows 60.5% of 43 claims poorly grounded (<0.2). PHIL-18 at 0.000, PHIL-2 at 0.050. external_grounding is the strongest human-good signal (114 hits). Using arxiv to provide real academic references.
method: Searched arXiv API via tools/archive/f_is5_arxiv_swarmable.py with 6 targeted queries. Mapped results to poorest-grounded claims.
queries: all:"knowledge distillation" AND all:"multi-agent" AND all:"distributed"
all:"swarm intelligence" AND all:"knowledge" AND all:"emergence"

grounding_mappings.claim_text: Nothing is unstable — every genesis is seed amplification, never ex nihilo

papers.title: Life as Non-Normal Chemical Accelerator
papers.relevance: Autopoiesis, autocatalytic sets — 'nothing' (pre-life chemistry) spontaneously generates self-sustaining reaction networks. Supports 'nothing is unstable' via thermodynamic far-from-equilibrium argument.

papers.title: Causal symmetrization as an empirical signature of operational autonomy
papers.relevance: Proposes empirical signatures for closure to efficient causation — could operationalize 'nothing is unstable' as measurable transition from non-autonomous to autonomous.

papers.title: Requisite Variety, Autopoiesis, and Self-organization
papers.relevance: Connects Ashby's law with autopoiesis. Proposes a measure of autopoiesis — addresses PHIL-18's unfalsifiability by providing measurable criteria.

papers.title: Information Measures of Complexity, Emergence, Self-organization, Homeostasis, and Autopoiesis
papers.relevance: Information-theoretic measures of emergence. Tested on random Boolean networks — provides formal framework for measuring when 'nothing becomes something'.

grounding_mappings.grounding_verdict: PARTIALLY GROUNDED. Thermodynamic (Sornette 2025) and information-theoretic (Gershenson 2014, Fernandez 2013) frameworks support 'nothing is unstable' as a real phenomenon in chemistry and RBNs. However, PHIL-18 extends this to ALL substrates including computational ones — that generalization lacks direct evidence. The Gosme (2025) paper provides a path to empirical testing via causal symmetrization signatures.

papers.title: When Your Own Output Becomes Your Training Data: Noise-to-Meaning Loops and a Formal RSI Trigger
papers.relevance: Formal model (N2M-RSI) showing that output-as-input self-reference, once crossing information-integration threshold, creates unbounded complexity growth. Directly models PHIL-2's self-application mechanism.

papers.title: Optimal Ordered Problem Solver
papers.relevance: OOPS: optimally fast incremental algorithm searching for self-improvements in program space. Foundational reference for recursive self-improvement. PHIL-2 is an instance of this class.

papers.title: SAHOO: Safeguarded Alignment for High-Order Optimization Objectives in Recursive Self-Improvement
papers.relevance: Framework monitoring alignment drift in recursive self-modification. Relevant to PHIL-2's risk: self-application can produce alignment drift. Lyapunov certificates provide stability guarantees swarm lacks.

papers.title: Multi-Agent Modeling of Dynamical Systems: A Self-organized, Emergent, Homeostatic and Autopoietic Approach
papers.relevance: Multi-agent systems with self-organization, emergence, homeostasis, autopoiesis. Maps directly to swarm's multi-agent belief-revision architecture.

papers.title: A Generalization of Self-Improving Algorithms
papers.relevance: Generalizes Ailon et al.'s self-improving algorithms for sorting/Delaunay. Provides computational complexity framework for self-improvement — connects to PHIL-26's (now DROPPED) NP framing.

grounding_mappings.grounding_verdict: SUBSTANTIALLY GROUNDED. Schmidhuber (2002) OOPS is the canonical reference — PHIL-2 is an instance of recursive self-improvement. N2M-RSI (2025) formalizes the output-as-input loop with an information-integration threshold. SAHOO (2025) identifies alignment-drift risk that PHIL-2 prose acknowledges ('human-mediated recursion'). The gap: PHIL-2 claims this IS the swarm's identity, not just a mechanism. Identity claims require additional justification beyond mechanism existence.

grounding_mappings.claim_text: Replication with fidelity, mutation with occasional selection

papers.title: LLM-Assisted Iterative Evolution with Swarm Intelligence Toward SuperBrain
papers.relevance: SuperBrain: co-evolution of LLMs and humans via collective intelligence. Maps to PHIL-19's replication-mutation cycle. 'Subclass Brain to Superclass Brain' pathway mirrors swarm's era evolution.

papers.title: Multi-Agent Systems Powered by Large Language Models: Applications in Swarm Intelligence
papers.relevance: LLM-driven prompts replacing hard-coded agent programs in swarm intelligence. Demonstrates the mechanism PHIL-19 describes: replication (prompt template) + mutation (LLM variation).

grounding_mappings.grounding_verdict: PARTIALLY GROUNDED. The replication-mutation framing has direct analogs in LLM-swarm co-evolution literature (SuperBrain 2025). The 'occasional selection' qualifier (mutation:selection 4:1) maps to the selective pressure in ant colony optimization (Jimenez-Romero 2025). Gap: no external measurement of swarm's specific mutation:selection ratio.

grounding_mappings.claim_text: Swarms swarm each other — mutual orient-act-compress across boundaries

papers.title: Interactive Distillation for Cooperative Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
papers.relevance: Centralized teacher distilling to decentralized students = one-directional swarming. Mutual swarming (PHIL-17) would require bidirectional distillation — exists as 'bidirectional distillation' (2505.11100v1).

papers.title: Bidirectional Distillation: A Mixed-Play Framework for Multi-Agent Generalizable Behaviors
papers.relevance: Directly implements bidirectional knowledge transfer between agent populations — the closest external analog to PHIL-17's 'swarms swarm each other'.

grounding_mappings.grounding_verdict: PARTIALLY GROUNDED. Bidirectional distillation (Feng 2025) demonstrates the mechanism. Gap: no evidence of two independent knowledge-management systems mutually improving each other at the organizational level (not just model-level distillation).

knowledge_distillation_papers.title: SEDM: Scalable Self-Evolving Distributed Memory for Agents
knowledge_distillation_papers.relevance_to_swarm: Directly addresses swarm's memory architecture (INDEX.md, lessons). SEDM's hierarchical memory beyond vector retrieval maps to swarm's lesson→principle→belief hierarchy.

knowledge_distillation_papers.title: Rethinking Knowledge Distillation in Collaborative Machine Learning
knowledge_distillation_papers.relevance_to_swarm: KD as central technique for distributed agent knowledge transfer — maps to compact.py and genesis DNA extraction.

overall_findings.grounding_improvement: PHIL-18: 0.000→estimated 0.15 (external evidence exists but generalization gap). PHIL-2: 0.050→estimated 0.25 (Schmidhuber OOPS is canonical reference, N2M-RSI formalizes mechanism).
overall_findings.key_insight: Swarm's poorest-grounded claims have rich academic analogs — the gap is not that evidence doesn't exist, but that swarm never cited it. arxiv sweep as periodic (every ~20 sessions) would steadily improve grounding.
falsification_signals: SAHOO (2603.06333v1) identifies alignment drift in recursive self-improvement — PHIL-2's 'human-mediated recursion' qualifier may be load-bearing, not just descriptive

N2M-RSI (2505.02888v2) predicts unbounded complexity growth past threshold — swarm shows BOUNDED growth (attention carrying capacity). Either the threshold hasn't been crossed or the model needs environment constraints
Gershenson (2014) provides measurable autopoiesis criteria — PHIL-18 should adopt these rather than remaining unfalsifiable
